title: oxalis hirsutissima
intro: Oxalis hirsutissima, also known as hairy wood sorrel, is a perennial herb belonging to the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae) and is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant's leaves and stems contain oxalic acid, which has been used for its medicinal properties to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The leaves of Oxalis hirsutissima have been traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to take advantage of its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In addition to its medicinal uses, the plant's leaves can be used as a natural herb in salads, adding a tangy flavor and nutritional value.
